entity.summary: Ukrainian centralized cryptocurrency exchange that announced an orderly shutdown in 2025 after domestic access was blocked during an investigation and regulatory/legal pressure in Ukraine.
entity.notes: Death date follows the announced complete platform cessation date of 2025-04-30. The shutdown was presented as orderly, but the underlying trigger involved court-ordered blocking and Bureau of Economic Security investigation context, so death_reason is classified as regulation.

events.title: KUNA launched in Ukraine
events.description: KUNA operated as a Ukrainian cryptocurrency exchange and became one of the country's best-known local trading platforms.
events.notes: Year-level launch date based on public background references.

events.title: Closure process began after domestic blocking and investigation context
events.description: KUNA's founder announced that the exchange would wind down commercial operations after domestic blocking connected to Ukrainian enforcement and court action.
events.notes: Regulatory/legal trigger event preceding the final shutdown date.

events.title: KUNA completed platform shutdown
events.description: KUNA's closure roadmap set April 30, 2025 as the complete cessation date for the platform after earlier deposit, exchange, and withdrawal deadlines.
events.notes: Used as death_date because public closure reporting identifies this as the complete cessation date.

evidence.title: KUNA company background reference
evidence.notes: Used only for basic background, exchange identity, and launch-year cross-checking.

evidence.title: The founder of the oldest cryptocurrency exchange in Ukraine, Kuna, announced its closure
evidence.notes: Used for the founder's closure letter, withdrawal roadmap, and domestic blocking/regulatory context.

evidence.title: Ukrainian crypto exchange Kuna ceases operations
evidence.notes: Used for the initial shutdown announcement and Ukrainian-market context.

evidence.title: Ukraine's Oldest Cryptocurrency Exchange KUNA Shuts Down After Court Ban
evidence.notes: Used for shutdown and court-ban framing.
